merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established when the American Consul at Brazzaville, Alan W. Lukens, presented his letter of credence as Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on August 13, 1960, when the American Consul at Brazzaville, Alan W. Lukens, presented his letter of credence as Charge d'Affaires.

merged_events.event_description: Embassy Bangui was established with Alan W. Lukens as resident Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Embassy Bangui was established on February 10, 1961, with Alan W. Lukens as resident Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: Initial U.S. diplomatic presence established in the Central African Republic upon independence, with Alan Wood Lukens as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim presenting credentials.

evidence.quote: Alan Wood Lukens, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1960-08-13.

merged_events.event_description: Embassy Bangui reopened after closure in 1997.

evidence.quote: Perry arrived at post on January 26, 1999, and assumed charge. He presented credentials on February 1, 1999, and the Embassy reopened on June 1, 1999.

matched.notes: CSV date 1961-02-10 matches RDCR Event 1 exactly. POCOM Event 2 gives 1960-08-13 (credential presentation date) and labels it Embassy, but RDCR explicitly states 'Embassy Bangui was established on February 10, 1961,' so RDCR wins per source priority. No discrepancy.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Diplomatic relations established when the American Consul at Brazzaville, Alan W. Lukens, presented his letter of credence as Chargé d'Affaires. This predates the formal establishment of Embassy Bangui by about six months.
missing_from_csv.notes: High-confidence RDCR event. This represents the initial establishment of diplomatic relations before the Embassy was set up. Should be added as a row preceding the Embassy row.
High-confidence POCOM event. Embassy closure is a significant status change missing from the CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Embassy Bangui reopened on June 1, 1999, after having been closed since March 1997.
missing_from_csv.notes: High-confidence POCOM event. Embassy reopening is a significant status change missing from the CSV.
